key_insight: Detonation-sprayed Al2O3-based coatings on stainless steel achieved a strong α-Al2O3-dominant structure with improved hardness, corrosion resistance, and wear performance, with performance depending on barrel filling level.
engineering_relevance: The study identifies detonation-spray settings that can be tuned to produce durable protective ceramic coatings for wear- and corrosion-critical components.
method: Deposited Al2O3-based oxide coatings on 10Kh18N9 austenitic stainless steel by detonation spraying.
Compared barrel filling levels of 53%, 58%, and 63% to study coating formation and properties.
Measured phase composition, microhardness, elastic modulus, corrosion behavior, and tribological wear against ZrO2 and 100Cr6 counterbodies.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports α-Al2O3 as the dominant phase (77–80%), peak microhardness of 1362.3 HV at 58% filling, and the best corrosion resistance at 63% filling with Icorr = 0.00725 A/cm².
watch_item: The best hardness, corrosion resistance, and wear behavior occur at different filling levels, so process optimization must balance competing property targets rather than assume one setting is universally optimal.
paper_title: DEVELOPMENT AND CHARACTERIZATION OF WEAR-RESISTANT AL₂O₃-BASED COATINGS PRODUCED BY DETONATION SPRAYING
